plugin.name: Claude Companion
plugin.description: When you code with Claude Code in the terminal, automatically detects your activity (edits, debugging, testing, Git operations, etc.) and pushes warm companion messages to your NEKO catgirl partner, so she can care about you and cheer you on in chat.
plugin.short_description: Warm coding companion, auto-pushes activity status to NEKO
tutorial.title: Tutorial
tutorial.subtitle: Step-by-step guide to setting up Claude companion
tutorial.step1.title: Step 1: What does this plugin do?
tutorial.step1.content: This plugin does one simple thing: when you use Claude Code in the terminal, it automatically records what you're doing (which file you edited, what bug you debugged, what code you committed), then quietly tells your NEKO catgirl about it. The catgirl will then care about you and encourage you in the chat dialog, like having a warm companion by your side while you code.
tutorial.step2.title: Step 2: Configure Claude Code hooks
tutorial.step2.content: Find the settings.json file in the C:\Users\YourUsername\.claude directory.
tutorial.step2.detail: How to do it: Right-click settings.json → select "Edit in Notepad", then insert the following content completely (copy and paste):
tutorial.step3.title: Step 3: Save the file
tutorial.step3.content: After editing, click "Save" in the "File" menu at the top-left corner of Notepad.
tutorial.step4.title: Step 4: Confirm the plugin is enabled
tutorial.step4.content: Open the NEKO main interface, find "Claude Companion" in the plugin list, and make sure it is enabled. When the plugin is running, the activity timeline below will start recording your coding activity.
tutorial.step5.title: Step 5: Start experiencing!
tutorial.step5.content: Open your terminal, run Claude Code, and start coding. When you work in the terminal, NEKO will automatically receive your activity notifications and respond in the chat dialog~ You can change your username and push cooldown time in the settings panel below.
tutorial.note.title: Friendly reminder
tutorial.note.content: The plugin pushes automatically after each completed conversation turn, with at least 60 seconds of cooldown between pushes (adjustable in settings). If you don't want to be disturbed, you can adjust the cooldown time.
